title: Attestplane Obligation Registry Entry
description: Schema for a single regulatory-obligation entry as loaded by attestplane.obligations.registry. Each entry maps a specific regulatory article / paragraph to the Attestplane evidence event types and fields that satisfy (or are designed toward) it. Locked by ADR-0008 cross-reference and the claim-safety triad in docs/policy/.

framework.description: Short stable framework name, e.g. 'EU AI Act', 'DORA', 'NIS2', 'GDPR', 'ISO 42001', 'NIST AI RMF'.

article.description: Article number as a string (article numbering may be alphanumeric in some frameworks). Numeric leading required.

paragraph.description: Paragraph / sub-paragraph identifier, e.g. '1', '2(a)', '3(d)'. Free-form short string; preserve the regulator's notation.

obligation_id.description: Stable dotted identifier; lower_snake_case segments. Format <framework_slug>.<article_slug>.<paragraph_slug>.<aspect_slug>. Renaming an obligation_id is a breaking change.

regulatory_text.description: Verbatim citation of the regulatory text. MUST be a quotation from the source regulation; paraphrase belongs in a separate 'paraphrase' field if needed (not part of v1 schema). Use single quotes around inline phrasing; preserve original capitalisation.

required_evidence_fields.description: Top-level EventDraft / AuditEvent fields that MUST be populated for an event to count as evidence satisfying this obligation. Field names follow snake_case from types.py / types.ts.

optional_evidence_fields.description: Fields that, when present, strengthen the evidence. Verifier MAY surface their absence as a warning.

event_type_mapping.description: v1 evidence event_type strings whose occurrence contributes evidence for this obligation. Each string MUST be a member of attestplane.event_types.ALL_EVENT_TYPES_V1; verifier raises on unknown strings.

verifier_expectation.description: One-sentence description of what a verifier checks. Imperative voice. Example: 'Every eval_event has matched_input_ref populated.'

implementation_status.description: Locked enum. MUST be one of four values; any other value is rejected by the schema and by the claim-safety triad. See docs/policy/forbidden_claims.md § Obligation-registry implementation_status.

legal_disclaimer.description: Plain-language disclaimer that the entry is a mapping target, not a compliance opinion. Repeated per entry so a verifier output that quotes a single entry still carries the disclaimer.

source_citation.description: Canonical bibliographic citation: regulation name, official identifier, adoption date, publication reference. Must be sufficient for a regulator or auditor to locate the source.

notes.description: Optional implementation notes; never used for legal interpretation.
